message:
feat(fr-persist): compact-type encoder selection in encode_rdb_with_options (br-frankenredis-91kt)

Closes the open r73v sub-task (a-ext): "byte-identical re-emit on save"
for the canonical-shape side. fr-persist::encode_rdb has historically
emitted only the canonical (non-compact) RDB type tags
(RDB_TYPE_LIST=1, _SET=2, _HASH=4, _ZSET_2=5), even for shapes upstream
would prefer the compact forms (LIST_QUICKLIST_2=18, SET_INTSET=11,
SET_LISTPACK=20, HASH_LISTPACK=16, ZSET_LISTPACK=17). That meant our
re-emit was state-equivalent (per the live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb
gate from r73v sub-task a) but byte-divergent from upstream-produced
dump.rdb on every shape that fits a compact threshold.

This commit:

* Adds `CompactRdbThresholds` (defaults match Redis 7.2 vanilla:
  hash_max_listpack_entries=512/value=64, set_max_intset_entries=512,
  set_max_listpack_entries=128/value=64, zset_max_listpack_entries=128/
  value=64, list_max_listpack_size=8192).
* Adds `RdbEncodeOptions { compact: Option<CompactRdbThresholds> }`.
* Adds `encode_rdb_with_options(entries, aux, options) -> Vec<u8>`
  that dispatches based on the supplied options. Existing
  `encode_rdb` becomes a thin wrapper passing `Default::default()`
  (compact=None) so prior callers see byte-identical output.
* Ports the listpack + intset encoder helpers from fr-store::dump_key
  into fr-persist (fr-store can't be a dep of fr-persist; the dep
  arrow goes the other way) — `encode_intset_blob`,
  `encode_listpack_strings_blob`, `encode_listpack_entry`,
  `encode_listpack_backlen`. Both sides round-trip through the shared
  `decode_listpack` / `decode_intset_members` readers.
* Adds compact-shape selection helpers:
  - `encode_compact_set_intset` — accepts only members whose canonical
    decimal form round-trips byte-identically (rejects "+1", "01",
    " 1", etc. which upstream's intset path also refuses).
  - `encode_compact_set_listpack` — len ≤ threshold + per-member
    byte cap.
  - `encode_compact_hash_listpack` — len + per-field/value byte caps.
  - `encode_compact_zset_listpack` — len + per-member byte cap +
    NaN-rejection (upstream `d2string` can't represent NaN).
  - `encode_compact_list_quicklist2` — single-PACKED-node form for
    lists fitting in `list_max_listpack_size`, with per-PLAIN-node
    fallback when individual elements exceed the budget.
* 6 new tests:
  - `encode_rdb_default_options_emit_canonical_type_tags` — pin the
    backward-compat contract (default `encode_rdb` keeps emitting
    type bytes 1/2/4/5).
  - `encode_rdb_with_compact_options_emits_compact_type_tags` — pin
    the new contract (small shapes select 11/16/17/18/20).
  - `encode_rdb_compact_falls_back_to_canonical_above_thresholds` —
    pin the threshold logic (600-entry int set → RDB_TYPE_SET, 100-byte
    hash value → RDB_TYPE_HASH, etc.).
  - `encode_rdb_compact_set_intset_rejects_non_canonical_integers` —
    "+1"/"01"/" 1" must NOT trigger intset (would lose original bytes).
  - `encode_rdb_compact_zset_listpack_rejects_nan` — NaN scores fall
    back to RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2.
  - `compact_encoder_round_trips_upstream_shaped_seeds` — the actual
    91kt acceptance gate: load each compact-encoded fuzz corpus seed
    (from 00bd2fb), decode, re-encode with compact options, and
    assert the same compact type tag is selected for each shape.

Validated via rch: cargo test -p fr-persist 150/150; cargo clippy
-p fr-persist --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo check
--workspace --all-targets clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
